# ha-frontend 20260527.5 — LCARS Theme Impact Analysis

## Release Notes

- Statistics graph card editor: add sub editor (@ildar170975 - #52182)
- Match the card style of apps repo to installed (@timmo001 - #52407)
- Add customize toggle to media player source and sound mode feature editors (@piitaya - #52414)
- Patch tinykeys v4 to make it compatible with older iOS versions (@jpbede) - #52420)
- Add maintenance my redirect (@timmo001 - #52442)
- Fix hui-editor search (@karwosts - #52453)
- Fix yaml entity autocomplete (@karwosts - #52475)

---

## HIGH IMPACT

No high-impact breaking changes were identified in this release. No CSS custom properties were renamed or removed, no web component element names changed, no shadow DOM structure was restructured, and no card-mod-targeted selectors were invalidated by these changes.

---

## MEDIUM IMPACT

### Component: `hui-editor` (`src/panels/lovelace/hui-editor.ts`)

**What changed:** The Lovelace full-config YAML editor added three new layout rules to the `ha-yaml-editor` element inside `LovelaceFullConfigEditor`:

```css
ha-yaml-editor {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  height: 100%;
  min-height: 0;   /* added */
}
```

The `display: flex`, `flex-direction: column`, and `min-height: 0` rules were added alongside the pre-existing `height: 100%`. This changes `ha-yaml-editor` from a default block-layout element to a flex column container with overflow-safe `min-height: 0`.

**What ha-lcars rule is affected:** The preamble sets `code-editor-background-color: black` as a global theme variable (in `&base`). The `card-mod-config-yaml` section does not currently inject any CSS directly targeting `ha-yaml-editor` or its internal layout. However, the global `code-editor-background-color: black` variable is consumed by `ha-yaml-editor`'s internal shadow DOM. The new flex layout means the editor's internal `ha-code-editor` child will now stretch to fill available height via flex rather than relying on explicit height cascades.

**Impact on LCARS:** If the theme or any user card-mod rule previously relied on `ha-yaml-editor` being a block element (e.g., to set a fixed height or to use `height: 100%` on it expecting block behavior), the flex change could cause layout shifts in the YAML editor dialog/panel. In practice, since LCARS does not appear to inject CSS directly into `hui-editor`'s shadow DOM, the risk is cosmetic — the editor may now correctly fill its container rather than potentially overflowing or collapsing, which is actually an improvement.

**Suggested fix:** No immediate fix required. If the YAML editor's black background or sizing appears incorrect after upgrade, add a targeted rule in `card-mod-config-yaml`:

```yaml
"hui-editor $": |
  ha-yaml-editor {
    flex: 1;
    min-height: 0;
  }
```

This is a precautionary measure only and is not expected to be needed unless a visual regression is observed.

---

### Component: `hui-media-player-sound-mode-card-feature-editor` / `hui-media-player-source-card-feature-editor`

**What changed:** Both media player card feature editors (`hui-media-player-sound-mode-card-feature-editor` and `hui-media-player-source-card-feature-editor`) had their internal schema generation refactored. The `sound_modes` and `sources` select fields (previously rendered as `ha-form` `select` selectors with `multiple: true, mode: "list", reorder: true`) are now delegated to a shared `customizable-list-feature` sub-editor. This changes the internal component structure rendered inside these editor dialogs — what was previously a flat `ha-form` select list is now rendered through `customizable-list-feature`, which likely renders a different DOM structure (a sub-editor component with its own shadow root and form elements).

**What ha-lcars rule is affected:** The `card-mod-dialog-yaml` block in `preamble.yaml` applies broad variable overrides to dialog hosts:

```yaml
card-mod-dialog-yaml: |
  .: |
    :host {
      --lcars-primary-text: var(--lcars-text-light);
      --card-background-color: var(--lcars-settings-card-color);
      --mdc-text-field-fill-color: transparent;
      --mdc-text-field-ink-color: var(--lcars-primary-text);
      ...
    }
```

These CSS custom properties are inherited and will still propagate into the new sub-editor's shadow DOM via custom property inheritance. However, if the `customizable-list-feature` sub-editor introduces new structural elements (e.g., new `ha-form` instances, toggle chips, or list-item components) that rely on `--md-*` tokens rather than `--mdc-*` tokens, the LCARS variable overrides for those elements may not apply.

**Suggested fix:** Monitor the visual appearance of the media player sound mode and source card feature editors in the Lovelace card editor dialog after upgrading. If text contrast or field backgrounds appear incorrect, inspect the shadow DOM of `customizable-list-feature` and add targeted overrides in `card-mod-dialog-yaml` if new `--md-sys-*` or `--ha-color-*` tokens are used by the new sub-editor component.

---

## LOW IMPACT

### Component: `hui-statistics-graph-card-editor`

**What changed:** The statistics graph card editor (`hui-statistics-graph-card-editor`) received a substantial expansion adding a sub-editor panel. This adds new internal form structure: a `hui-sub-element-editor` (or similar pattern) is likely introduced for per-stat entity configuration. New `ha-form` fields, `ha-entity-picker` instances, and schema rows will appear inside this editor dialog.

**What ha-lcars rule is affected:** None directly. The broad `card-mod-dialog-yaml` and `card-mod-config-yaml` variable cascades will apply automatically to any new form fields via custom property inheritance.

**Suggested fix:** No fix needed at this time. Optionally, if the new sub-editor panel renders with incorrect background colors or text contrast, add targeted rules in `card-mod-config-yaml` scoped to `hui-statistics-graph-card-editor $` or `hui-statistics-graph-card-editor hui-sub-element-editor $`. This is purely optional enhancement.

---

### Component: General — No Variable Renames

**What changed:** No `--ha-*`, `--mdc-*`, `--md-*`, `--ha-card-*`, or layout token renames occurred in this release across the four diffed files.

**Suggested fix:** No fix needed. All existing LCARS variable mappings (`--mdc-text-field-fill-color`, `--mdc-text-field-ink-color`, `--ha-card-border-radius`, `--ha-card-background`, `--wa-form-control-value-color`, etc.) remain valid.
